label: UNIFIED-THROW covariance, 5D (pt,pz,Eavail,q3,W); PET frozen-reweighter (lower bound -- Tier-2 retraining not yet folded in)
verdict: VERDICT (UNIFIED throw): on the 10550 common 5D bins, median per-bin fractional uncertainty is 16.7% for PET (frozen-reweighter unified C_total, a LOWER BOUND) vs 11.9% for GBDT (re-unfolded adopted unified cov); median ratio 1.564, PET tighter in 23% of bins. The unified throw inflates the systematic over block-sum by 1.54x (GBDT) and 5.71x (PET). ASYMMETRY CAVEAT: the GBDT unified throw re-unfolds each throw (captures the retraining nonlinearity), whereas the PET unified throw uses a FROZEN reweighter, so the PET number OMITS its retraining-response term and can only rise once the Tier-2 study is folded in -- a PET<=GBDT reading here is therefore not yet conclusive.
